text:
Once upon a time in a quaint little town nestled between rolling hills and sprawling fields, there was a shopkeepers' daughter named Lucy. Despite never having worked outside her family's apple orchard because of the sheer difficulty of plucking apples from orchard fences—all the while reading endlessly from the few books her parents allowed—she was known among the townsfolk for her extraordinary gift for storytelling.

Every evening after the orchard chores were done, Lucy would retreat to her attic room, surrounded by towering bookshelves littered with novels and folklore. There, she'd weave tales of adventure, magic, and mystery that captivated every child who dared to listen.

One crisp autumn evening, after the harvest festival colors turned to brown, Lucy sat by the fireplace, weaving furiously into her latest story. Her characters were woodland creatures negotiating with woodland spirits. The moment she concluded her tale, she knew something was off; the moonlight seemed too intense.

Stumbling over her steps, she rushed downstairs towards
